$comment: Per-repo OIDC allowlist for ingest step 2 (DR-035 B1).
Every ingest worker verifies the source CI run's OIDC claims against this
pinned set BEFORE any bundle is verified or rendered. A mismatch on issuer,
subject, OR workflow_ref crashes the worker (fail-closed).
issuer  — the GitHub Actions OIDC issuer (fixed for all GH-hosted runners).
subjects — allowed `sub` claim values. GitHub OIDC `sub` for a tag release is
`repo:OWNER/REPO:ref:refs/tags/vX.Y.Z`. The trailing-glob entries
(`...:ref:refs/tags/*`) match the release-tag family; the worker
treats a single trailing `*` as a prefix match.
workflowRefs — allowed reusable-workflow refs. GitHub sets the
`job_workflow_ref` / `workflow_ref` claim to
`OWNER/REPO/.github/workflows/FILE@REF`.
manifestTag — OPTIONAL per-repo override for manifest resolution. When set,
the ingest resolver fetches `report-manifest.json` from the
fixed Release tag `releases/download/<manifestTag>/` instead of
`releases/latest/download/`. Needed where `releases/latest` is
polluted by unrelated releases: on ccp
(claude-code-plugins-plus-skills), per-package npm releases
dominate — 100 of the 100 most recent releases are package
releases — so the evidence manifest lives on the rolling
`evidence-latest` tag instead.
ccp rationale: the repo was renamed to
`jeremylongshore/claude-code-plugins-plus-skills` (OIDC sub claims carry the
new name). Its evidence emit lands as a NEW push-main workflow named
`emit-evidence.yml` (sibling PR builds it); `release.yml` there is
dispatch-only + GITHUB_TOKEN-suppressed, so tag-based claims
(`refs/tags/*`) are unsatisfiable — the pin is `refs/heads/main`.
OPERATOR-CONFIRMABLE: where the exact release-workflow filename or ref could
not be derived from the repo at authoring time, the canonical
`release.yml@refs/heads/main` form is used as a placeholder and flagged
`operatorConfirmed: false`. Confirm against each repo's actual
.github/workflows/ before first production ingest, then flip to true.
jrig rationale: a SECOND ingest channel on j-rig-skill-binary-eval (iaj
covers its tag-based release channel). The emitter is a nightly cron
(`nightly-skill-evals.yml`, PR j-rig-skill-binary-eval#215) running j-rig
eval over an 8-skill marketplace roster; scheduled runs execute on the
default branch, so the OIDC ref is `refs/heads/main`. Rolling
`evidence-latest` tag because per-package npm releases pollute
`releases/latest` (same pollution as ccp). Pinned BEFORE the emitter's
first run (the ccp #51→#52 sequence): `operatorConfirmed: false` until the
first live manifest verifies.
qmd rationale: push-to-main workflow (`emit-evidence.yml`) attesting the
governed brain's provenance-integrity + govern-decision + retrieval-ratchet
CI evals. The repo cuts NO Releases at all, so the rolling `evidence-latest`
tag is the only manifest home. Pinned before first emit;
`operatorConfirmed: false` until the first live manifest verifies.
Renamed 2026-07-19 to `jeremylongshore/bobs-big-brain-registrar` — OIDC
claims carry the NEW slug after the rename, so both old- and new-slug
subjects/workflowRefs are pinned during the transition (additive; the
old-slug entries are dropped once the first new-name manifest verifies).
ICOS is intentionally ABSENT — struck from the supervision tree per the
cross-tier policy (DR-035). 8 repos exactly: iec, iel, iah, iaj, iar, ccp,
jrig, qmd.
